title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
description: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
description: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
description: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
description: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
description: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
description: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.

title: VCs are betting billions on AI’s next wave, so why is OpenAI killing Sora?
description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back. That tension is everywhere […]

title: OpenAI shuts down Sora while Meta gets shut out in court
description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back. That tension is everywhere […]

title: David Sacks is done as AI czar — here’s what he’s doing instead
description: Sacks will be much further from the power center in Washington than since the outset of this second Trump administration.

title: Anthropic wins injunction against Trump administration over Defense Department saga
description: A federal judge has ordered that the Trump administration rescind recent restrictions it placed on the AI company.

title: You can now transfer your chats and personal information from other chatbots directly into Gemini
description: Google is launching "switching tools" that, just as it sounds, will make it easier for users of other chatbots to switch to Gemini.

title: Wikipedia cracks down on the use of AI in article writing
description: The site, whose policies are subject to change, has struggled with the issue of AI-generated writing.

title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
description: It's only the latest of several side projects that the AI startup has ditched over the past week.

title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
description: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren want the Energy Information Administration to gather more details about how data centers use power — and how that affects the grid.

title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
description: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
description: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
description: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

title: Mistral releases a new open source model for speech generation
description: The model, which lets enterprises build voice agents for sales and customer engagement, puts Mistral in direct competition with the likes of ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and OpenAI.

title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
description: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?
